text:
There once was a little old lady who lived near an apple tree.

Every year she would go there to admire their wonderful shiny apples.

One year she discovered that some apples were bigger than others.

That year she saved extra money so she could buy all the biggest ones.

And they were delicious.

She sat under that tree.

Now she just sits there sometimes.

They call that a treasure tree.

They say the biggest ones are good luck.

They even talk about them on local radio.

They love that little old lady.

So maybe someday she’ll find a big enough one.

Then she’ll have lots.

That would be wonderful.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
